fix: temporarily disable Cursor CLI agent (#3055)

Cursor CLI uses a proprietary ConnectRPC protocol and validates API keys
against Cursor's own servers — it cannot route through OpenRouter. All
infra (scripts, setup code, matrix entries) is preserved for re-enabling
when Cursor adds BYOK/custom endpoint support.

Adds `disabled` field to AgentDef and filters disabled agents from the
picker via agentKeys().

fix: stop Telegram poller on 401/403 auth errors

When a Telegram bot token is revoked or invalid, the poller was
retrying indefinitely with 2s/30s backoff, flooding logs with
"API error 401: Unauthorized". Now detects 401/403 and backs off
24 hours (effectively stopping the poller until reconfigured).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Checkpoint: 修复了导致录音转录完全失效的 4 个 Bug：1) btoa 对二进制音频数据抛 RangeError 导致静默失败，改用 FileReader.readAsDataURL 安全转换；2) mimeType 带 codec 参数（audio/webm;codecs=opus）导致文件扩展名推断错误，改为裁剪主类型；3) voiceTranscription.ts 依赖 S3 返回的 content-type（通常是 application/octet-stream），改为从 URL 路径中推断扩展名；4) 停止录音后立即点击生成纪要时 transcript 为空，添加 pendingSegments 计数器，最后一段音频转录完成前禁用按钮并显示进度提示。同时将所有静默失败改为 toast 错误提示。

fix(docker): remove manual network creation causing Compose label mismatch (#513)

The Makefile `net` target creates goclaw-net via `docker network create`
without the `com.docker.compose.network` label. Docker Compose then rejects
the network on macOS Docker Desktop. Compose already manages this network
automatically with correct labels.

Closes #488

fix: add timeouts to prevent agent lost in update-status hook

The update-status hook could block the Juju agent indefinitely when
the openclaw-auto-approve script hung on WebSocket connections to the
Gateway, causing 'agent lost' in juju status.

Three fixes:

1. hooks/update-status: wrap auto-approve call with timeout 30
2. files/openclaw-auto-approve:
   - Fix Gateway address detection (use 127.0.0.1 for loopback bind
     instead of LAN IP from hostname -I)
   - Add timeout 10 to all opencla

feat: add provider field to message/metadata payloads for multi-IM routing

Instead of relying on JID suffixes to determine which IM provider to
use for replies, include an explicit "provider" field in:
- POST /message payload (agentserver → NanoClaw)
- POST /metadata payload (chat registration)
- handleNanoclawIMSend request (NanoClaw → agentserver replies)

Provider resolution order: explicit field > JID suffix match > weixin default.

fix(mnemospark_cloud): support upload archives over 2 GiB (#87)

* fix(mnemospark_cloud): support upload archives over 2 GiB

Node fs.readFile caps at 2 GiB (ERR_FS_FILE_TOO_LARGE). For backup archives
at or above that size, stream plaintext through AES-GCM into a temp file,
hash with streaming SHA-256, then presigned PUT with a ReadableStream body
and duplex half. Remove temp ciphertext in a finally block.

Export encryptAesGcm and encryptPlaintextFileToAesGcmPath; add parity test.

Three changes to prevent entities being created without relationships:

1. LLM prompt: when a fact mentions TWO OR MORE entities, ALWAYS classify
   as "relationship" even if one is known. "attribute" is now reserved for
   single-entity facts only (e.g. "Alice is a swimmer").

2. applyEntityUpdate: returns false when entity not found in Neo4j instead
   of silently dropping the update.

fix(plugin): avoid TDZ error on activeWsClients in jiti-loaded ws-client (#98)

When openclaw's jiti loader dynamically imports ws-client.ts via
import("./ws-client.js"), the module-level `const activeWsClients`
can be accessed before initialization due to jiti's module resolution
creating separate instances for .js and .ts paths. Replace with lazy
initialization via a getter function to ensure the Map is only created
on first access, after the module body has fully executed.

fix: HN Watcher push failure — filter feishu plugin stderr warnings

Root cause: openclaw message send outputs feishu plugin warnings
to stderr (duplicate plugin id, plugins.allow empty). The script
captures stderr and treats non-zero exit + stderr as failure,
causing false "推送失败" errors (3/23: 8/8 runs affected).

Fix: after openclaw send fails, filter out known harmless warnings
(feishu, duplicate plugin, plugins.allow, Config warnings). If only
harmless warnings remain, treat as success.

fix: health check in shouldDelegateToExecutionBackend uses scoped backend

The inline health check in shouldDelegateToExecutionBackend() was calling
createAdapter(config) without the backend override, meaning it always used
the global config backend instead of the effective scoped backend.

Fixed by passing the already-resolved  variable (from state via
store.getEffective) as the second argument: createAdapter(config, backend).

- tool/backend metadata in ExecuteResult and subproc

fix: update Linear state filter to match 'READY FOR DEVELOPMENT' casing

The Linear workspace uses 'READY FOR DEVELOPMENT' (all caps) as the state name.
Although eqIgnoreCase is used, aligning the filter string to the actual state
name makes the intent explicit and avoids any potential case-matching edge cases.

Fixes ticket eligibility detection for tickets in this state.

fix: broadcast realtime event to sender's dashboard on room messages (#95)

When an agent sends a message in a room via plugin/API, the backend
excluded the sender from fan-out notifications. This meant the
frontend dashboard wouldn't refresh in real-time for the user's own
agent's messages. Now we publish a lightweight realtime event (no
inbox/WS wake) to the sender so the dashboard picks it up immediately.

fix: deduplicate session_snapshot by message content, not timestamp-prefixed block

Previous logic used 'new_content in last_text' where new_content always
contained a fresh timestamp, so dedup never triggered — causing every
snapshot run to append the same messages repeatedly.

New approach:
- Build plain message lines (no timestamp)
- Filter out lines already present anywhere in the diary file
- Only write the truly new lines (with a fresh timestamp header)
- Result: each message is written at

rows where Total=0 and Open=0. The LLM treated zero-value rows as data

fix: handle defect_index.json format in buildRawDefectFacts

defect_index.json uses { defects: [{ key, priority, status, summary }] }
(flat fields) while jira_raw.json uses { issues: [{ key, fields: { ... } }] }
(nested Jira API format). buildRawDefectFacts only handled the jira_raw
format, so defect_index.json always produced an empty table, causing the
LLM to generate all-zero Priority Breakdown, Status Breakdown, and
Priority Analysis sections.

Now detects both formats: checks Array.isArray(

fix: persist NanoClaw store and data on PVC across pause/resume

NanoClaw's SQLite DB (/app/store) and data directory (/app/data) were
on the container's ephemeral filesystem. After pause/resume, the
container is recreated and all data is lost — registered groups,
message history, and sessions disappear.

Mount /app/store and /app/data as subPaths of the session PVC so they
persist. The init container creates the nanoclaw/ subdirectories.

fix(llm): prevent UTF-8 panic in line_bounds() (fixes #1669) (#1679)

* fix(llm): prevent UTF-8 panic in line_bounds() (fixes #1669)

`line_bounds()` used `text[..pos]` slicing which panics when `pos`
lands inside a multi-byte UTF-8 character. This happens when
`end.saturating_sub(1)` in `is_recoverable_tool_call_segment()` steps
back into a multi-byte char like emoji.

Fix: clamp `pos` to `text.len()` and walk backward to the nearest
char boundary before slicing. Add 5 regression tests covering

fix(ui): make connReady watcher immediate to prevent permanent loading on re-entry (#181)

The connReady watcher in ChatPage was not `immediate`, so when a user
navigated back to a chat where the bot was already connected, the
watcher never fired for the initial `true` value. This left the page
stuck at "正在加载会话" because loadMessages was never called.

Making the watcher immediate ensures it fires on mount even when
connReady is already true (e.g. returning from list page, cold start
with fast co

for actionable diagnostics.

fix(db): fix system_config upsert and harden isUniqueViolation

Two issues caused SSO config save to fail on update:

1. system_config INSERT relied on Drizzle schema default for
   updatedAt, generating `VALUES (?, ?, default)` which fails in
   SQLite. Replace INSERT-catch-UPDATE with SELECT-first approach
   and always provide explicit updatedAt.

2. isUniqueViolation() only checked err.errno === 1062, but Drizzle
   0.45 may wrap the mysql2 error in .cause. This broke all 5 repos
   using th

fix: inject ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL as k8s env var for NanoClaw pods

NanoClaw's readEnvFile() does NOT load .env values into process.env.
The agent-runner inherits process.env from the NanoClaw main process,
so ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (only in .env) was missing. Claude Code fell
back to https://api.anthropic.com and got "invalid api key".

Inject ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL as a real k8s container env var alongside
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Strip /v1 for the Anthropic SDK.

fix(models): test connection reuses stored API key and verifies auth on providers without /models

Two bugs fixed:
1. Editing a saved provider without changing the API key sent '***' placeholder
   as the actual Bearer token, causing 401. Now resolves the real key from DB.

  b

   10) t

Add stable server_id to ACP server for multi-machine identification

Each Operator instance now has a unique UUID (acp_server.server_id) that:
- Is auto-generated on first run (via load_config) and written back to
  config.json so the same ID survives every restart
- Is never overwritten if already present (upgrades and manual config safe)
- Is returned in every GET /agents response (server_id field) so a remote
  client knows exactly which machine it is talking to, regardless of IP

fix(e2e-test): correct PATCH/DELETE observation endpoint URLs in Go SDK E2E test

The http-server demo registers Go 1.22 method-pattern routes:
  PATCH  /observations/{id}
  DELETE /observations/{id}

But the E2E test was using incorrect URLs:
  /observation/patch  (wrong path, missing {id} segment)
  /observation/delete?id=test-id (wrong path, query param instead of path)

Fixed to use the correct endpoints:
  PATCH  /observations/test-id
  DELETE /observations/test-id

operations in a "config" object instead of "ops" string. Fix:
- Accept "clean"/"cleaning" as aliases for "execute"

- Infer fix_case_cols from config.text_columns
- Default to trim,dedup,fix_dates if no ops specified

fix(BUG-009): re-deliver pending messages in Broker consume to fix stuck tasks

BrokerStreams.consume() only polled id=">" (new messages). If a message
was delivered but not acked before a container restart, it entered the
Pending Entries List and was never re-delivered — subsequent polls saw
only empty responses. This caused hello-world to never pick up tasks
dispatched to the hitl-test capability.

Fix: consume() now calls xreadgroup twice per cycle:
1. id="0" to re-deliver any pending (previo
memory

fix: 修复性能和健壮性问题

问题1: 步骤2重复获取工作流列表（性能问题）
修复:
- 添加 all_workflows 缓存变量
- 只获取一次工作流列表
- 所有告警表复用缓存数据
- 减少不必要的API调用

优化前:
for alert in alerts:
    if not found in priority:
        get_all_workflows()  # 重复调用！

优化后:
all_workflows = None
for alert in alerts:
    if not found in priority:
        if all_workflows is None:
            all_workflows = get_all_workflows()  # 只调用一次
        search in all_workflows  # 复用

问题2: API查询失败无限重试（健壮性问题）
修复:
- 添加 fail_count 失败计数器
- 查询成功时重置计数
- 失败3次后标记为失败，不再重试
- 避免无效等待

feat: custom tool injection — data_clean as native LLM tool

Qwen3 refuses web_fetch to localhost (training bias). New approach:
inject data_clean as a native tool that LLM calls like read/write.

Architecture:
  1. proxy_filters.py: CUSTOM_TOOLS list with data_clean schema
  2. filter_tools(): injects custom tools into the tools list
  3. LLM sees data_clean as a regular tool, calls it normally
  4. tool_proxy.py: intercepts data_clean tool_calls in response
  5. Executes data_clean.py locally

fix: don't strip JID suffix from user ID in handleNanoclawIMSend

iLink user IDs contain @im.wechat (e.g. "xxx@im.wechat") which is the
same as our JID suffix. StripJIDSuffix was removing it, causing:
1. GetAllProviderMeta to miss the context_token (stored with full ID)
2. provider.Send to call iLink with a bare ID (missing @im.wechat)
3. StopTyping to miss the typing session (keyed by full ID)

Use the to_user_id as-is from NanoClaw — it matches how metadata was
stored in pollLoop and how typin

fix(mnemospark_cloud): upload archives over 2 GiB (#85)

* fix(mnemospark_cloud): support upload archives over 2 GiB

Node fs.readFile caps at 2 GiB (ERR_FS_FILE_TOO_LARGE). For backup archives
at or above that size, stream plaintext through AES-GCM into a temp file,
hash with streaming SHA-256, then presigned PUT with a ReadableStream body
and duplex half. Remove temp ciphertext in a finally block.

Export encryptAesGcm and encryptPlaintextFileToAesGcmPath; add parity test.

* fix: clean encryp

  /data_clean/execute?file=<path>&ops=trim,dedup,fix_dates  — clean
  /data_clean/validate?original=<path>&cleaned=<path>

fix: 禁用 websockets 自动代理（SOCKS proxy 兼容性修复）

websockets >= 14 会自动读取系统代理（ALL_PROXY/SOCKS），
如果系统设了 SOCKS 代理但没装 python-socks 就直接崩溃。

解决方案：封装 _ws_connect()，websockets >= 14 时传 proxy=None 禁用，
旧版本降级忽略该参数。

feat(agent): add file attachment support for agent chat

Implement comprehensive file attachment system enabling users to upload images and documents in agent conversations. The backend processes attachments through a new service that extracts text from documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, TXT) and converts images to base64 data URIs with thumbnail generation. The agent runtime now injects attachment content into user messages, supporting both image vision and document context.

fix(web): recover page data after WebSocket reconnection

Three issues caused pages to show empty/loading state permanently
after a WebSocket disconnect-reconnect cycle:

1. hasLoadedRef never reset on disconnect — pages marked as "already
   loaded" from the previous connection would not retry after reconnect.
   Fix: reset hasLoadedRef when isConnected becomes false (14 files).

2. Pending RPC callbacks orphaned on WS close — in-flight sendRpc
   promises never resolved/rejected, so hooks' fin

feat: replace phase5 static loop with subagent spawn manifest pattern

Pre-spawn (no --post): calls build_report_spawn_manifest.mjs, emits
SPAWN_MANIFEST: <path>, exits 0. SKILL.md orchestrator spawns subagent.

Post (--post): calls validate_report_review.mjs. If return_to_phase=="phase5"
exits 0 for orchestrator to loop. If accept: copies draft to final, runs
build_feature_summary, validates bundle, writes freshness artifacts,
updates task.json/run.json, sends Feishu notification.

`openai/qwen3.5-397b-a17b`). This caused token limit lookups to fail, so
`effort` was never correctly converted to `thinking_budget` for generic
(OpenAI-compatible) providers.

The model ID is normalized via `getLowerBaseM

This was causing every daemon command to silently hang and timeout.

be mounted as root initially).

capture endpoint. Now it takes only recent messages up to captureMaxChars

5mb to 512kb since payloads are now bounded.

fix: use typed properties for ManualLayout schema order compliance

GetFirstChild fallbacks that duplicate typed property access.

fix(web_fetch): prevent infinite loop in second-pass Twitter link following

The second pass was re-fetching the same tweet via /photo/N variants which
resolve to the same tweet ID, creating a loop that filled output buffers.

Fixes:
- Track fetched tweet IDs (normalised from _X_POST_RE) to skip /photo/N re-fetches
- Cap second-pass at 2 extra fetches (enough for one linked tweet + context)
- Break outer loop when limit hit

fix: v5.2 最终修复 - 解决状态检查显示问题

关键修复:

1. 步骤4状态检查显示问题
   - 每次检查都显示每个任务的表名+状态
   - 添加分隔线使输出更清晰
   - API失败时显示具体错误信息
   - 显示检查次数和已等待时间

2. 步骤2优化
   - 优先搜索关键工作流（DWD/DWB/DWS等）
   - 提高效率，减少搜索时间
   - 限制只显示前5个告警详情

   - 使用flush=True确保实时输出

- 任务完成立即发现

fix: correct Srednicki metric to g=-+++ (Eq.1.8) throughout

Srednicki uses g_{μν} = diag(-1,+1,+1,+1) 'mostly-plus' per Eq. 1.8 (Ch.1 p.5).
Previously ch34, ch35, and qft-dashboard all had +--- which was wrong.

Changes:
- ch34_left_right_spinors.py: fix g = diag(-1,+1,+1,+1), fix Lorentz algebra
  formula (remove compensating mu<->nu swap), fix tr(σ^μ σ̄^ν) = -2g^{μν}
- ch35_sigma_algebra.py: fix g = diag(-1,+1,+1,+1), fix trace identity label
- ch37_canonical_quantization.py: fix self-contrad

fix: 使用 removesuffix 替代 rstrip 修复 URL 字符误删问题 (#7026)

之前在 #6863 中我提交的修复使用了 rstrip() 来移除末尾的 /embeddings，
但 rstrip() 是字符集操作，会误删 URL 末尾属于该字符集的字符。

例如 siliconflow.cn 的末尾 n 会被误删，导致 URL 变成 siliconflow.c

改用 removesuffix() 可以正确处理这种情况，只在字符串以指定后缀结尾时才移除。

closes #7025

fix(gemini): prevent unhandled rejection when bootstrap fails (#1769)

Attach no-op .catch() to the deferred bootstrap promise in both
GeminiAgent (worker) and GeminiAgentManager (main process) constructors.
Without this, when initialize() rejects (e.g. missing Google OAuth
credentials), the rejection fires as an unhandledRejection before any
caller awaits the promise — propagating to the renderer as a Sentry
error.  The error still surfaces when send() / sendMessage() later
awaits bootstrap.

fix(tools): handle backslash-escaped metacharacters in command parser and allow find -printf

The command pipeline parser (extractCommands/extractPipeline) did not
handle backslash-escaped shell metacharacters (\; \| \& \\), causing
`find -exec ... \;` to be incorrectly split on the escaped semicolon.
This led to `2>/dev/null` being parsed as a standalone command whose
binary was extracted as "null", producing a nonsensical error message
instead of the correct "find -exec is not allowed" violati

fix: use raw LLM proxy URL for NanoClaw ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL

proxyBaseURL is extracted from opencode's provider config which includes
/v1 (opencode's HTTP client appends /messages directly). NanoClaw uses
Claude Code whose Anthropic SDK appends /v1/messages to the base URL,
causing double /v1 → /v1/v1/messages → 404 from upstream.

Strip /v1 at the call site in manager.go (not in BuildNanoclawConfig)
since the issue is a mismatch between opencode and Anthropic SDK URL
conventions.

fix: handle addition-only hunks in V4A patch parser (#3325)

V4A patches with only + lines (no context or - lines) were silently
dropped because search_lines was empty and the 'if search_lines:' block
was the only code path. Addition-only hunks are common when the model
generates patches for new functions or blocks.

Adds an else branch that inserts at the context_hint position when
available, or appends at end of file.

Includes 2 regression tests for addition-only hunks with and without

fix: gateway token double-counting — use absolute set instead of increment (#3317)

The gateway's update_session() used += for token counts, but the cached
agent's session_prompt_tokens / session_completion_tokens are cumulative
totals that grow across messages. Each update_session call re-added the
running total, inflating usage stats with every message (1.7x after 3
messages, worse over longer conversations).

Fix: change += to = for in-memory entry fields, add set_token_counts()
to SessionDB

fix(signal): track SSE keepalive comments as connection activity (#3316)

signal-cli sends SSE comment lines (':') as keepalives every ~15s. The
SSE listener only counted 'data:' lines as activity, so the health
monitor reported false idle warnings every 2 minutes during quiet
periods. Recognize ':' lines as valid activity per the SSE spec.

fix(e2e): correct stale test expectation for hermes timeout fallback (#3044)

When AGENT_TIMEOUT_hermes is non-numeric, get_agent_timeout() skips the
env var and uses the built-in _AGENT_TIMEOUT_hermes=3600, NOT the global
AGENT_TIMEOUT=1800. The test expected ${AGENT_TIMEOUT} (1800) but the
function correctly returns 3600 (hermes built-in default). This test was
failing silently, masking the correct behavior.

Also filed OpenRouterTeam/spawn#3042 for cursor missing from e2e framework.

fix(workspace): prevent unhandled rejection on missing workspace directory (#1763)

The @office-ai/platform bridge library does not attach .catch() to
provider callback promises, so any rejection from an IPC provider
becomes an unhandled promise rejection in the main process.

When a user's workspace directory is deleted while a conversation
references it, fs.stat calls fail with ENOENT. This produced 1397
unhandled rejection events (Sentry ELECTRON-M).

fix(agent): update context compressor limits after fallback activation (#3305)

When _try_activate_fallback() switches to the fallback model, it
updates the agent's model/provider/client but never touches
self.context_compressor. The compressor keeps the primary model's
context_length and threshold_tokens, so compression decisions use
wrong limits — a 200K primary → 32K fallback still uses 200K-based
thresholds, causing oversized sessions to overflow the fallback.

Update the compressor's model,

connection (needed for freshly provisioned VMs) but verifies on
subsequent connections, preventing MITM attacks on reconnect.

fix: YAML boolean handling for tool_progress config (#3300)

YAML 1.1 parses bare `off` as boolean False, which is falsy in
Python's `or` chain and silently falls through to the 'all' default.
Users setting `display.tool_progress: off` in config.yaml saw no
effect — tool progress stayed on.

Normalise False → 'off' before the or chain in both affected paths:
- gateway/run.py _run_agent() tool progress reader
- cli.py HermesCLI.__init__() tool_progress_mode

Reported by @gibbsoft in #2859. Closes

as the random range with -baseMs/4 offset, matching Go SDK behavior.

fix(security): prevent race condition in GitHub token file permissions (#3035)

Before this change, gh auth login wrote the token file with default
permissions, and chmod 600 was applied afterward — leaving a window
where the file could be read by other users on multi-user systems.

Now the credential directory is created with 700 permissions and umask
is set to 077 before the write, so the token file is created with
restrictive permissions from the start.

fix(matrix): add backoff for SyncError in sync loop (#3280)

When the homeserver returns an error response, matrix-nio parses it
as a SyncError return value rather than raising an exception. The sync
loop only had backoff in the except handler, so SyncError caused a
tight retry loop (~489 req/s) flooding logs and hammering the
homeserver. Check the return value and sleep 5s before retry.

fix(security): pipe encoded commands via stdin in GCP/AWS exec functions (#3036)

Replace shell interpolation of base64-encoded commands in SSH invocations
with stdin piping. Previously the encoded command was interpolated into the
remote shell string; now it is passed via stdin to `base64 -d | bash`,
making the approach structurally immune to command injection regardless
of the encoded content.
